prompt:
Design a Library Operations & Enrollment Management Suite to support a public or corporate library running fully offline, covering daily circulation, scheduled program enrollment, and governed content publishing. The React web interface is optimized for staff workstations and provides role-based navigation for Administrator, Operations Staff, and Content Moderator, with each screen showing only permitted menus, actions, and data scope. Staff can search and manage Reader Profiles, viewing library card status (active, frozen, blacklisted), current holdings, and borrowing history, with privacy fields like phone numbers displayed masked by default and revealed only to authorized roles after a step-up check. Operations Staff can manage holdings at the copy level by scanning or typing a barcode to update location, shelf position, and circulation status, and can run stocktake sessions that highlight variances and flag anomalous copies for follow-up holds. The system supports bulk import/export for readers and holdings using Excel/CSV templates, with an interactive preview that highlights errors before commit and allows clean export for audits. For scheduled programs (e.g., workshops), staff configure enrollment windows (for example, open 03/15/2026 9:00 AM–03/22/2026 5:00 PM), capacity limits, prerequisites, and whitelist/blacklist rules; enrollment actions provide immediate feedback on schedule conflicts, duplicate enrollment during add/drop, and remaining seats, then confirm a successful atomic enrollment. After a transaction or program completion, staff can capture ratings and tagged feedback, route appeals to arbitration, and review issue analytics linked to site performance KPIs and blacklist rules. Managers can run configurable business reports with filters and Excel/CSV export, including occupancy rate, RevPAR, and revenue mix aggregated by time, room type, and channel, with visibility constrained by role and assigned branch.

The backend uses Go with Echo to implement a decoupled RESTful API consumed by the React frontend over the local network or a single-machine deployment, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for users, roles, permissions, readers, holdings, enrollments, moderation items, audits, and reports. Authentication is strictly local username/password with salted hashing, server-side sessions that expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, CAPTCHA after 3 failed attempts, and account lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed logins; all privilege checks enforce RBAC plus resource-level permissions at the menu/API and data-scope level (e.g., branch-based row filtering). Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest using an application-managed key stored locally, and masking rules are applied at the API layer based on caller permissions; security auditing records logins, failed attempts, record exports, moderation decisions, and admin changes with timestamp, workstation identifier, and before/after values. Bulk imports run in a staged transaction: files are validated for format, required fields, and referential integrity, deduplication rules (e.g., barcode uniqueness and reader ID uniqueness) are applied, completeness thresholds are computed, and any row-level errors trigger a full rollback with a downloadable error report. Enrollment and add/drop use transactional locks to guarantee capacity and conflict validation with atomic commit, preventing race conditions when multiple operators enroll simultaneously. Reporting queries are pre-aggregated nightly on a local schedule and recalculated on demand for selected date ranges, and all exports are logged to support offline compliance reviews without relying on any third-party services.
